**Main orchestrator script** (recommended)\n25\t- `version-drift-check.py` - Python pipeline that wires together all components\n26\t- `github-release-fetcher.py` - Fetches releases from GitHub API\n27\t- `find-armor-deployments.py` - Discovers ARMOR deployments in declarative-config\n28\t- `compare-version-drift.py` - Compares deployments against releases\n29\t- `armor-version-drift-check.cron` - Cron job definition\n30\t- `setup-version-drift-schedule.sh` - Setup script to install cron job\n31\t\n32\t### Usage\n33\t\n34\tRun the check manually:\n35\t```bash\n36\t./scripts/check-version-drift.sh\n37\t```\n38\t\n39\tWith custom thresholds:\n40\t```bash\n41\t./scripts/check-version-drift.sh --releases 5 --days 60\n42\t```\n43\t\n44\tOutput JSON format:\n45\t```bash\n46\t./scripts/check-version-drift.sh --json\n47\t```\n48\t\n49\tWrite report to file:\n50\t```bash\n51\t./scripts/check-version-drift.sh --output report.json\n52\t```\n53\t\n54\tSort by specific field:\n55\t```bash\n56\t./scripts/check-version-drift.sh --sort-by cluster\n57\t./scripts/check-version-drift.sh --sort-by correctness\n58\t```\n59\t\n60\t### Options\n61\t\n62\t- `--releases N` - Flag deployments N or more releases behind (default: 50)\n63\t- `--days N` - Flag deployments N or more days behind (default: 30)\n64\t- `--json` - Output machine-readable JSON instead of human-readable format\n65\t- `--output FILE` - Write report to file (in addition to stdout)\n66\t- `--sort-by FIELD` - Sort output by field: cluster, releases, days, correctness (default: correctness)\n67\t- `--config FILE` - Use configuration file (JSON)\n68\t- `--slack-webhook-url URL` - Send alert to Slack webhook\n69\t- `--dry-run` - Run without sending actual alerts (for testing)\n70\t- `--help` - Show help message\n71\t\n72\t### Exit Codes\n73\t\n74\t- `0` - All deployments within thresholds\n75\t- `1` - orchestrator script** (recommended)\n- `version-drift-check.py` - Python pipeline that wires together all components\n- `github-release-fetcher.py` - Fetches releases from GitHub API\n- `find-armor-deployments.py` - Discovers ARMOR deployments in declarative-config\n- `compare-version-drift.py` - Compares deployments against releases\n- `armor-version-drift-check.cron` - Cron job definition\n- `setup-version-drift-schedule.sh` - Setup script to install cron job\n\n### Usage\n\nRun the check manually:\n```bash\n./scripts/check-version-drift.sh\n```\n\nWith custom thresholds:\n```bash\n./scripts/check-version-drift.sh --releases 5 --days 60\n```\n\nOutput JSON format:\n```bash\n./scripts/check-version-drift.sh --json\n```\n\nWrite report to file:\n```bash\n./scripts/check-version-drift.sh --output report.json\n```\n\nSort by specific field:\n```bash\n./scripts/check-version-drift.sh --sort-by cluster\n./scripts/check-version-drift.sh --sort-by correctness\n```\n\n### Options\n\n- `--releases N` - Flag deployments N or more releases behind (default: 50)\n- `--days N` - Flag deployments N or more days behind (default: 30)\n- `--json` - Output machine-readable JSON instead of human-readable format\n- `--output FILE` - Write report to file (in addition to stdout)\n- `--sort-by FIELD` - Sort output by field: cluster, releases, days, correctness (default: correctness)\n- `--config FILE` - Use configuration file (JSON)\n- `--slack-webhook-url URL` - Send alert to Slack webhook\n- `--dry-run` - Run without sending `find-armor-deployments.py`\n2. **Fetching**: Fetches latest GitHub releases using `github-release-fetcher.py`\n3. **Comparison**: Compares deployed versions against releases using `compare-version-drift.py`\n4. **Flagging**: Flags deployments needing attention based on thresholds\n\n### What It Checks\n\nThe script scans ARMOR deployments across all clusters in `jedarden/declarative-config` and:\n1. Reads the deployed ARMOR image tag from each deployment file\n2. Compares against the current ARMOR version (from `VERSION` file)\n3. Calculates version drift (versions behind, days behind)\n4. Checks for correctness/security releases between versions\n5. Flags deployments needing attention\n\n### Clusters Checked\n\n- iad-ci\n- iad-kalshi\n- rs-manager\n- ord-devimprint\n- iad-native-ads\n- iad-acb\n\n### Thresholds\n\nBy default, deployments are flagged if:\n- More than 50 versions behind current\n- More than 30 days behind current\n- Any correctness/security releases missed\n\nThese thresholds can be adjusted in the script.\n\n### Scheduling\n\nTo schedule daily checks (if cron is available):\n```bash\nbash scripts/setup-version-drift-schedule.sh\n```\n\nThe check runs daily at 9:17 AM (avoiding :00/:00 load spikes).\n\nLogs are written to `logs/version-drift-check.log`.\n\n### Slack Integration\n\nThe drift check can send alerts to a Slack webhook when drift is detected.\n\n**Setting up Slack notifications:**\n\n```bash\n# Set the webhook URL as environment variable\nexport SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL=\"https://hooks.slack.com/services/YOUR/WEBHOOK/URL\"\n\n# Or pass directly\npython3 scripts/version-drift-check.py --slack-webhook-url $SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL\n\n# Or add to config file\necho '{\"slack_webhook_url\": \"https://hooks.slack.com/services/YOUR/WEBHOOK/URL\"}' > config/drift-config.json\n```\n\n**Testing Slack integration:**\n\n```bash\n# Test without sending actual alerts\npython3 scripts/version-drift-check.py --slack-webhook-url $SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL --dry-run\n```\n\n**Slack Alert Format:**\n\n- 🚨 **Red** - Correctness drift detected (bug/security fixes missed)\n- ⚠️ **Orange** - Regular drift detected (version or time thresholds exceeded)\n- ✅ **Green** - All deployments up to date\n\n**Example Slack message:**\n\n```\n🚨 ARMOR Version Drift Check Report\nCRITICAL: 1 deployment(s) with correctness drift\n\nTotal Deployments:    6\nWith Drift:           2\nWith Correctness Drift: 1\nThresholds:           >50 releases, >30 days\n\n🔴 iad-kalshi: 0.1.13 behind by 29 releases (45 days) - CORRECTNESS DRIFT\n🟡 rs-manager: 0.1.13 behind by 29 releases (45 days)\n```\n\n### Configuration\n\nCreate a `config/drift-config.json` file to customize defaults:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"releases_threshold\": 50,\n  \"days_threshold\": 30,\n  \"declarative_config_path\": \"~/declarative-config\",\n  \"github_repo\": \"jedarden/ARMOR\",\n  \"sort_by\": \"correctness\",\n  \"slack_webhook_url\": null,\n  \"dry_run\": false\n}\n```\n\n**Configuration Parameters:**\n\n| Parameter | Type | Description |\n|-----------|------|-------------|\n| `releases_threshold` | integer | Flag if N+ releases behind (default: 50) |\n| `days_threshold` | integer | Flag if N+ days behind (default: 30) |\n| `declarative_config_path` | string | Path to declarative-config (default: `~/declarative-config`) |\n| `github_repo` | string | GitHub repo for releases (default: `jedarden/ARMOR`) |\n| `sort_by` | string | Sort field: `cluster|releases|days|correctness` (default: `correctness`) |\n| `slack_webhook_url` | string | Slack webhook URL for alerts (or use `--slack-webhook-url`) |\n| `dry_run` | boolean | Skip actual alerts (for testing) |\n\n**Environment Variables:**\n\n- `SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL` - Fetch releases from GitHub\n2. Find all ARMOR deployments in declarative-config\n3. Compare deployments against releases\n4. Generate report (console + file output)\n5. Optionally send alerts to Slack webhook\n\nUsage:\n    python3 scripts/version-drift-check.py [--config CONFIG_FILE] [--json] [--output OUTPUT_FILE]\n                                            [--slack-webhook-url URL] [--dry-run]\n\nExamples:\n    # Run with default settings\n    python3 scripts/version-drift-check.py\n\n    # Run with JSON output for monitoring systems\n    python3 scripts/version-drift-check.py --json --output /tmp/drift-report.json\n\n    # Run with Slack notifications\n    python3 scripts/version-drift-check.py --slack-webhook-url $SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL\n\n    # Dry-run mode (test without sending alerts)\n    python3 scripts/version-drift-check.py --slack-webhook-url $SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL --dry-run\n\"\"\"\n\nimport argparse\nimport json\nimport os\nimport subprocess\nimport sys\nfrom datetime import datetime\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Dict, List, Any\nimport urllib.request\nimport urllib.error\n\n\n# Default configuration\nDEFAULT_CONFIG = {\n    \"releases_threshold\": 3,\n    \"days_threshold\": 30,\n    \"declarative_config_path\": \"~/declarative-config\",\n    \"github_repo\": \"jedarden/ARMOR\",\n    \"output_file\": None,\n    \"sort_by\": \"correctness\",\n    \"slack_webhook_url\": None,\n    \"dry_run\": False\n}\n\n\ndef load_config(config_path: str = None) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"Load configuration from file or use defaults.\"\"\"\n    config = DEFAULT_CONFIG.copy()\n\n    if config_path and Path(config_path).exists():\n        with open(config_path, 'r') as f:\n            user_config = json.load(f)\n            config.update(user_config)\n\n    # Expand tilde in paths\n    if 'declarative_config_path' in config:\n        config['declarative_config_path'] = os.path.expanduser(config['declarative_config_path'])\n\n    return config\n\n\ndef send_slack_notification(webhook_url: str, drift_result: Dict, config: Dict) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Send drift check results to Slack webhook.\"\"\"\n    if not webhook_url:\n        print(\"No Slack webhook URL configured - skipping notification\", file=sys.stderr)\n        return False\n\n    summary = drift_result.get('summary', {})\n    total = summary.get('total_deployments', 0)\n    with_drift = summary.get('with_drift', 0)\n    with_correctness_drift = summary.get('with_correctness_drift', 0)\n\n    #
